lines: Quartermaster Ruth pins the ledger open with one flat hand.
"No seal, no ration. No writ, no powder. No, a heroic face is not a seal."

effects.log: Ruth enters one field issue under your authority.

choices.label: Copy an issue seal into the margin

effects.log: Your copied seal enters Ruth's ledger before she turns the page.

lines: "One packet for a field writ: beans, dressing, candle stub, entry roll if Security has not misplaced the blessed things again."
"Anything more needs a dead officer's signature or a living officer's shame."

choices.label: Show her the false return weight

effects.log: Ruth strikes the false cart weight and initials the correction for Aquila.
Ruth returns the relief bundle to the road stock count.

choices.label: Show her the copied powder total

effects.log: Ruth sets the false powder total aside for comparison with Judith's slate.

lines: Ruth checks the relief stamp against the ration divider.
"Good. The food went where the stamp said, even if my porter did not. I will hang the empty cord from his ledger peg."
Ruth presses her thumb between each copied figure.
"Those totals are mine. The spaces belong to a price hand. I will compare the mark to Judith's slate before dusk."

choices.label: Leave the false total with Ruth

lines: "Judith is not official. Judith is useful. The Church has many words for sin and very few for useful."
"If she overcharges you, tell me. I will nod with great concern."
Ruth reads your posture before she reads the empty issue line.
"Fine. One field bundle, entered under your name. If Aquila asks why, stand exactly like that and waste her time instead of mine."
Your copied seal dries in the margin while Ruth counts beans with her back turned.
She closes the ledger on it. "If that mark is wrong, the wax will remember your thumb better than I do."
Ruth runs one nail down the return column and stops at the false total.
"Empty cart, full weight. Either Baruch carried a ghost or somebody used my ledger as a blanket. Tell the porter. I will tell Aquila with fewer rude words."
